customer_anxiety_profile: Low-urgency researcher. Balthazar is in early exploration mode, has no competing quotes, and has flagged schedule volatility ('my plans keep changing'). He is cooperative but not emotionally invested. His primary preference is asynchronous, low-commitment engagement — he asked for email delivery twice. He did, however, voluntarily block his calendar for the appointment, which is a partial commitment signal.
unresolved_concerns: Customer's preference for email delivery of the design — he asked twice and was told it would come after the appointment. Closer must honor this promise AND make the live call feel irreplaceable before sending anything.
Schedule volatility — customer explicitly said 'my plans keep changing.' No urgency anchor was established to protect the sit.

Unknown co-decision-maker — customer spoke to a second person (initials 'AB') during the call. Title co-owner status was never confirmed. Closer should verify at the top of the call whether anyone else should be present.
sensitive_topics_raised: Schedule instability — customer flagged this proactively; do not treat the appointment as firm without re-confirming at the top of the closer call.
exact_promises_rep_made: Specialist will call at 1PM tomorrow (the day after the setter call).
Call will be within five minutes of the scheduled time.
Design will be sent by email after the appointment for customer's records.
Appointment is by phone, not in person.
closer_should_know: The customer agreed to the appointment but has not been emotionally activated — he is still in research mode. His bill ($150-$200/month, ComEd, high in July/August) was captured but never flipped into urgency. The closer should open by anchoring that bill pain before presenting anything. Also: honor the post-meeting email promise, but make the live call feel irreplaceable before sending — otherwise the customer has no reason to show up.
